usage.exhausted_regex: (?i)(reached your (usage|weekly) limit|you(?:.?ve| have) reached your .{1,40}? limit\b.{0,40}?/usage-credits|usage limit reached|\b0%\s*(left|remaining)|out of (usage|credits)|upgrade to (a paid plan|continue))

interactive_prompts.name: cli_feedback_rating
interactive_prompts.regex: How's the CLI experience so far\?|Help us improve:
interactive_prompts.note: Quick feedback rating prompt from the CLI wrapper; '0' skips/cancels

interactive_prompts.name: trust_prompt
interactive_prompts.note: claude v2.1.193 folder-trust dialog: 'Quick safety check: Is this a project you created or one you trust?' with '❯ 1. Yes, I trust this folder' pre-highlighted and footer 'Enter to confirm'. The selection cursor ❯ is the SAME char as claude's REPL prompt marker, so the boot loop mistakes the dialog for a ready REPL and pastes the prompt INTO the dialog (lost → rc=81 artifact-timeout). tickDuringBoot dismisses it during boot before prompt delivery: a single Enter confirms the pre-highlighted 'Yes' (verified 2026-06-26, fresh-dir launch with --dangerously-skip-permissions). Regex anchors on the numbered menu-option line ('1. Yes, I trust this folder') so prose/agent output that merely mentions trusting a folder cannot false-match (tick also runs during the artifact-wait loop). once:true — fired a single time per launch. Same class as the Cycle-121 codex trust-modal premature-ready bug.

interactive_prompts.name: askuserquestion_multiselect
interactive_prompts.note: claude AskUserQuestion multiSelect: '[ ]' checkboxes + the 'Enter to select' menu footer. Verified 2026-05-26: Enter toggles the highlighted checkbox, Right moves to the Submit tab, Enter submits → recommended_or_first (keystrokes are PACED — see autoRespondInterKeyPause). Requires BOTH a '[ ]' checkbox AND the menu footer so a bare markdown checklist in agent output ('1. [ ] do thing') does NOT false-match, and so the rule keeps matching after the first checkbox toggles (other rows stay '[ ]') — enabling a retry if the first submit misses. MUST precede askuserquestion_select: a multi-select pane also contains that footer, so the checkbox rule must win (first-match). --dangerously-skip-permissions does NOT suppress this (a question is not a permission).

interactive_prompts.name: askuserquestion_select
interactive_prompts.note: claude AskUserQuestion single-select: first option pre-highlighted ('❯ 1.'); footer 'Enter to select · ↑/↓ to navigate · Esc to cancel'. Verified 2026-05-26: a single Enter selects the highlighted first option = recommended_or_first. Layer-2 net behind the prompt-prefix interactive policy (Layer 1).

interactive_prompts.name: auth_recheck
interactive_prompts.note: Bridge cannot complete OAuth; escalate to operator

interactive_prompts.name: model_deprecation_continue
interactive_prompts.note: Common edge case on model rollover; comma-separated key names

interactive_prompts.name: terminal_resize_redraw
interactive_prompts.note: Cosmetic redraw prompt; just press Enter

_notes_on_removed_patterns.fact_forcing_gate: REMOVED v12.1.5 after live-smoke forensics (workspace bridge-t7-G3mPzn). The ECC gateguard PreToolUse hook fires once per Write attempt and Claude recovers natively — it presents the 4 requested facts and retries the Write. Neither extend_timeout nor auto_respond is appropriate: extend_timeout doesn't help (Claude isn't slow, it's just sequencing tool calls), and auto_respond stacks identical messages at the prompt every poll-tick because the gate error remains visible in pane scrollback, interfering with Claude's own recovery. The correct bridge behavior is no rule — let Claude handle the hook natively. Re-introduce only with dedupe logic that suppresses re-fires within a single agent turn (v12.1.6+ design).
